description: This conceptual diagram illustrates a geodetic measurement network for precise positioning or ground motion monitoring. It depicts four peripheral ground stations, each represented as a tower with an antenna/sensor and its own local 3D Cartesian coordinate system (blue arrows), positioned on a flat, tilted plane. These stations are connected via orange dashed lines to a central point of interest, which is enclosed by a green ellipse. The central point also features its own local 3D Cartesian coordinate system, denoted by purple arrows. The arrangement suggests that measurements from the four distributed reference stations are used to determine or monitor the position, motion, or deformation characteristics of the central point. The green ellipse around the central point could represent measurement uncertainty, an error ellipse, or a deformation component. The diagram is labeled '(c)' in the bottom right corner.
